notes: META-HARNESS-114. The RING-LENGTH corroboration half of the Shift+Tab capture: same probe as the sibling permission-mode-cycle recording, but launched with '--dangerously-skip-permissions'. MEASURED RING LENGTH: 5 — the session starts on 'bypass permissions on' and Shift+Tab walks bypass permissions -> auto -> manual -> accept edits -> plan -> bypass permissions -> auto -> manual -> accept edits, so the lap closes on press 4 and repeats. 'bypass permissions' IS on the ring here, i.e. a bypass-enabling launch flag ADDS a rung rather than merely pre-selecting one. THIS REFINES, AND DOES NOT CONTRADICT, the note on the META-HARNESS-109 permission-mode-bypass fixture ('bypass is NOT on the cycle'): that session was launched with '--permission-mode bypassPermissions' and, by its own meta, pressed NO keys, so it recorded the 4-ring measured from a NORMAL launch. What is newly measured here is that under '--dangerously-skip-permissions' the ring is 5. Whether the other two bypass-enabling flags ('--permission-mode bypassPermissions', '--allow-dangerously-skip-permissions') also produce a 5-ring was NOT probed — see not_measured. NO CODE DEPENDS ON EITHER NUMBER: the downstream loop terminates by lap detection with a flat backstop precisely so a 4-vs-5 ring, or a future sixth rung, needs no code change. Recorded as corroboration only. bytes.raw ENDS BEFORE TEARDOWN, as in the sibling recording.
keystrokes: \x1b[Z x8 (1 candidate + 7 presses) — one rung per press, no exceptions
not_measured: Whether '--permission-mode bypassPermissions' and '--allow-dangerously-skip-permissions' also yield the 5-ring. Only '--dangerously-skip-permissions' was probed; the other two flags are assumed equivalent by name only, which is exactly the kind of assumption this fixture exists to avoid.

The kitty '\x1b[9;2u' encoding was not re-probed in this session — it was already measured working in the sibling permission-mode-cycle recording, and this run pinned '\x1b[Z' to keep the rung-per-press accounting unambiguous.
No 'Bypass Permissions mode' trust dialog was painted in this environment, so no dialog-dismissal keystrokes are in stdin.log; behaviour behind that dialog is unmeasured.
version_note: claude --version reported '2.1.218 (Claude Code)'. This task does NOT bump src/versions/versions.json.
